title: Egyptian Beef Dumplings
description: A festive and traditional Egyptian dish featuring tender beef dumplings in a rich broth. This dairy-free-friendly recipe is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Combine the flour, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
Gradually add the beef broth, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, until smooth.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Heat the beef fat or oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking up with a spoon as needed.
Add the dumplings to the pan and cook for 5 minutes, until golden brown on the bottom.
Reduce heat to low and add 2 cups of beef broth to the pan.
Bring the broth to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, until the dumplings are cooked through.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
